onboarding.title: Welcome to Habit Tracker
onboarding.subtitle: Build and maintain daily habits with streaks and progress tracking
onboarding.sampleDataDescription: We've added 6 sample habits with completion records and 5 categories to show you how tracking works.

steps.title: Define your habits
steps.description: Create habits with a name, frequency (daily or weekly), emoji, and target goals.

steps.title: Track completions & streaks
steps.description: Mark habits as done each day. Your streaks and completion history are tracked automatically.

steps.title: Use your AI agent
steps.description: Ask your agent to create habits, log completions, or show your streak stats.

reference.overview: A habit tracking system with daily checklists, streak counting, calendar heatmaps, and progress analytics. Organize habits by category and frequency.

concepts.name: Habits
concepts.description: The behaviors you want to track — each has a name, frequency, emoji, and belongs to a category.

concepts.name: Completions
concepts.description: Records of when you completed a habit on a specific date. These build your streaks and stats.

concepts.name: Categories
concepts.description: Groups like Health, Mindfulness, and Learning to organize your habits.

prompts: Create a habit: meditate for 10 minutes every morning
Show my current streaks for all habits
What's my completion rate for this week?
tips: Each habit is a JSON file in habits/ — completions go in completions/ with the naming pattern {habitId}-{date}.json
Categories are defined in categories.json — add your own to organize habits your way
Streaks are computed from completion records, so consistent logging is key

habits.title: No habits yet
habits.description: Start by defining the habits you want to build. Your AI agent can help you set them up.
habits.suggestedPrompt: Create these habits: exercise 30min daily, read 20 pages daily, drink 8 glasses of water
